items.myth: Constitutional AI means Claude literally re-reads a constitution document on every message.
items.reality: Principles shape training; inference uses learned behavior. Explicit multi-step critique may be a training technique, not something users trigger each chat.
items.example: A refusal on a harmful request feels immediate—you do not see a separate critique pass in the UI.

items.myth: CAI makes Claude infallibly ethical with no jailbreaks.
items.reality: Alignment reduces risk; adversarial prompts and edge cases remain. Safety is layered training plus policies, monitoring, and updates.
items.example: Red-team exercises still find bypasses that inform patches and policy tweaks.
